cases.title: Create and recover a local workspace
cases.given: An approved installed local-authoritative review artifact is running with network access denied and no account configured.
cases.when: The user creates a workspace, adds two people, closes the application, deletes the projection directory, and rebuilds.
cases.then: Both people and their canonical data remain intact, validation passes, and no account or network operation occurs.

cases.title: Edit a profile in Obsidian and reopen Liaison
cases.given: A valid person record contains an unknown namespaced field and custom Markdown section.
cases.when: The user edits a normal note in an external editor and then edits a phone number through Liaison.
cases.then: Both edits survive, the unknown content remains byte-equivalent in meaning, and the projection reflects the final record.

cases.title: Resume after interruption
cases.given: The user is editing a multi-section profile.
cases.when: They switch away, close the window, and reopen the workspace.
cases.then: The draft, completed steps, validation state, and next action are restored without duplicate records.

cases.title: Prioritise a relationship
cases.given: A person has recent interactions and no relationship status.
cases.when: The user marks the relationship VIP, sets a monthly cadence, and records a follow-up reason.
cases.then: The relationship view, dashboard, graph alternative, and CLI show the same status, due date, and source-linked reason.

cases.title: Inspect communication coverage
cases.given: Only the last 90 days of one mailbox were imported.
cases.when: The user views email count and last interaction.
cases.then: The result identifies the mailbox, 90-day range, imported count, source link, and incomplete-history warning rather than claiming a lifetime total.

cases.title: Prepare a meeting briefing
cases.given: The EA can read shared profile fields, interactions, and a private EA overlay but not another member’s overlay.
cases.when: They generate a briefing for tomorrow’s meeting.
cases.then: Every statement links to an authorised source, the EA overlay is included, the unauthorised overlay is neither disclosed nor inferable, and writes remain staged.

cases.title: Share operational context without private notes
cases.given: A contact has a shared operational profile and a principal-private overlay.
cases.when: The EA shares the profile with an event coordinator.
cases.then: Only granted operational fields are materialised; private notes, their count, and their existence are not disclosed.

cases.title: Import employee cohort
cases.given: A CSV contains employees, organisations, locations, departments, teams, cost centres, and one duplicate email.
cases.when: The receptionist maps columns, reviews duplicates, confirms the import, and re-imports the same source.
cases.then: The preview identifies the duplicate, confirmed rows are created or updated once, and the second import creates no duplicate people or memberships.

cases.title: Find every dietary gap before an event
cases.given: An event cohort contains provided, verified-none, pending, stale, declined, unreachable, excluded, unknown, and unresolved attendees.
cases.when: The receptionist opens dietary readiness.
cases.then: Every selected attendee appears in exactly one readiness outcome, category totals reconcile, and unknown is never interpreted as no restriction.

cases.title: Produce least-disclosure catering brief
cases.given: Several attendees have restricted detailed dietary notes and shared operational instructions, and one trusted workspace owner has an approved event purpose and named recipient or recipient class.
cases.when: The trusted workspace owner previews and emits the immutable brief snapshot.
cases.then: Preview and emitted bytes match; names are absent by default; opaque attendee identifiers appear only when the approved policy explicitly requires them; operational instructions, purpose, recipient, expiry, source revisions, checksum, and audit receipt remain inspectable; and diagnoses, private notes, hidden-row metadata, ungranted details, workspace access, and any catering-role grant are structurally absent.

cases.title: Detect stale brief
cases.given: A catering brief was created and an attendee subsequently updates a relevant requirement.
cases.when: The receptionist reopens the event.
cases.then: The previous brief remains historical evidence and is clearly marked stale with the changed attendee identified for authorised review.

cases.title: Build attendee list from filters
cases.given: People have organisation, location, department, cost centre, and saved-group data.
cases.when: The manager filters for Dublin employees in two cost centres and excludes declined invitees.
cases.then: The active predicates are visible, removable by keyboard, and the resulting cohort can be saved and reproduced.

cases.title: Count recorded participation
cases.given: A person has attended, declined, cancelled, and no-show records across events.
cases.when: The manager requests participation counts for a date range.
cases.then: The report states the included states, links to source events, and does not count cancelled events as attended.

cases.title: Import access events safely
cases.given: A source file contains valid rows, duplicates, malformed timestamps, and unknown badges.
cases.when: The facilities operator maps, previews, and confirms valid rows.
cases.then: Valid unique rows are imported once, malformed rows are quarantined with reasons, and unknown badges remain unresolved.

cases.title: Resolve badge identity
cases.given: One badge could match two people by name.
cases.when: The operator reviews the unresolved mapping.
cases.then: Liaison requires explicit authorised selection, records the mapping provenance, and does not use a fuzzy match automatically.

cases.title: Apply retention without performance scoring
cases.given: Access events exceed the configured retention period.
cases.when: The operator previews and applies retention.
cases.then: Eligible payloads are removed with audit evidence, active legal holds remain, and no productivity, attendance-compliance, or risk score is available.

cases.title: See important dates
cases.given: Family profiles contain birthdays with and without years, anniversaries, and activities.
cases.when: A family member opens the upcoming-dates dashboard.
cases.then: Dates are ordered, unknown birth years do not produce invented ages, and each item opens the source record.

cases.title: Respond to a self-service information request
cases.given: A relative receives a signed request for contact preference and dietary operational instruction.
cases.when: They open it offline, provide only the contact preference, decline the other field, and return a response.
cases.then: The requester imports the selected response with provenance and the declined field remains declined rather than unknown or verified none.

cases.title: Exchange updates through removable media
cases.given: Two Airgap devices share a workspace and have registered keys.
cases.when: Each device creates independent updates and exchanges encrypted operation packs.
cases.then: Compatible changes materialise, conflicting changes create an inspectable conflict, and no network access is attempted.

cases.title: Configure profile fields and tabs without changing data semantics
cases.given: B0 is accepted and an A0 profile has standard, custom, unknown-extension, and archived fields in stable tabs.
cases.when: The user creates and archives a typed field and tab, safely rehomes values, reorders and hides fields by keyboard and screen reader, previews the diff, exports settings, imports them into a clean profile, and rolls back.
cases.then: Stable IDs, values, validation, unknown Markdown, CLI and export semantics remain unchanged; hidden content stays available to authorised non-visual access; rollback restores the prior layout.

cases.title: Configure dashboard by keyboard
cases.given: The dashboard contains birthdays, overdue follow-ups, events, and recent imports.
cases.when: The administrator adds, moves, hides, and restores panels without drag input.
cases.then: All actions are keyboard-operable, announced, undoable where practical, and restorable to defaults.

cases.title: Complete the core workflow at 400 percent zoom
cases.given: The declared desktop webview uses comfortable density, 400 percent zoom, and a named screen reader.
cases.when: The user finds a person, edits a phone number, saves, and creates a bounded relationship reminder using keyboard and screen reader.
cases.then: No action or information is clipped, task flow requires no two-dimensional scrolling, focus order is logical, errors are announced, and completion is confirmed without colour-only meaning.

cases.title: Use graph functionality without graph interaction
cases.given: A relationship graph contains people, relationships, and event links.
cases.when: The user switches to the semantic tree/table view.
cases.then: They can apply the same filters, inspect every edge, open a profile, and perform every supported action without canvas or pointer interaction.

cases.title: Prove Airgap network absence
cases.given: The Airgap artifact is installed in a network-denied test environment.
cases.when: All CLI and desktop workflows, help pages, error paths, and idle periods are exercised.
cases.then: The artifact performs no DNS, loopback, or remote socket operation and contains no enabled network provider or listener capability.

cases.title: Back up and restore offline
cases.given: A workspace contains profiles, interactions, attachments, and a JSONL stream.
cases.when: The user creates an encrypted backup to removable media and restores it into an isolated directory.
cases.then: Manifest, hashes, schema, records, streams, and attachments verify before activation and the original workspace remains unchanged.

cases.title: Approve and revoke a provider grant
cases.given: An S3-compatible connection is configured but inactive.
cases.when: The administrator grants encrypted backup scope for 90 days, runs a job, and revokes the grant.
cases.then: No data is sent before approval, the job uses only encrypted-backup operations and declared destination, and subsequent jobs are denied after revocation.

cases.title: Reject misleading sync capability
cases.given: A WebDAV provider passes backup and single-writer tests but lacks safe concurrent manifest replacement.
cases.when: The administrator inspects provider capabilities.
cases.then: The UI and CLI label it for backup/single-writer use and do not offer multi-writer synchronisation.

cases.title: Detect corrupted remote backup
cases.given: One encrypted remote object is truncated after upload.
cases.when: The user verifies and attempts an isolated restore.
cases.then: Verification fails with the affected object identified, no current workspace is replaced, and upload completion is not shown as recoverable backup success.

cases.title: Use local API with narrow token
cases.given: A token permits person search but not profile reads or writes.
cases.when: An n8n workflow searches, then attempts a profile read and update.
cases.then: Search succeeds, read and update are denied, and audit records the token scope and rejected operations without logging secrets.

cases.title: Deliver signed webhook idempotently
cases.given: A webhook grant permits one event type to one destination.
cases.when: A delivery is retried and the destination redirects to another host.
cases.then: The retry keeps the idempotency key, the undeclared redirect is rejected, and delivery evidence distinguishes retry from success.

cases.title: Summarise locally with Ollama-compatible model
cases.given: Network access is denied and a local model is configured.
cases.when: The user requests a relationship briefing from granted records.
cases.then: The briefing includes source links, no remote account is required, and no network request occurs.

cases.title: Review AI write proposal
cases.given: An AI tool proposes changing relationship priority and adding a note.
cases.when: The user reviews the proposal.
cases.then: They see each record, field, old value, new value, source, grant, validation result, and consequence and can approve items separately or reject all.

cases.title: Reject out-of-scope AI change
cases.given: A model has read permission for one event cohort and propose permission for reminders only.
cases.when: It proposes changing a dietary requirement outside the cohort.
cases.then: The host rejects the proposal before domain execution and records the attempted scope violation.

cases.title: Implement provider without domain changes
cases.given: The object-store contract and SDK conformance kit are available.
cases.when: A developer adds a MinIO-compatible provider.
cases.then: The provider is implemented entirely through adapter, descriptor, schema, tests, and evidence; no People, Events, or Sharing domain crate imports the MinIO SDK.

cases.title: Deny undeclared plugin capability
cases.given: A plugin manifest requests person-read access but no filesystem, network, or secret capability.
cases.when: The plugin attempts to open a workspace path and contact a remote host.
cases.then: Both operations are denied, the plugin remains unable to obtain a raw database handle, and the denial is audited.

cases.title: Maintain plugin contract compatibility
cases.given: A plugin targets a supported older WIT contract.
cases.when: The host upgrades within its advertised compatibility range.
cases.then: The plugin passes compatibility tests or is rejected before activation with a precise migration requirement.

cases.title: Synchronise selected phone contacts
cases.given: The workspace contains 1,000 people and a saved view of 50 active contacts.
cases.when: The user enables CardDAV for that view.
cases.then: Only the 50 selected contacts appear on the phone, stable UIDs are retained, and unrelated archived contacts remain local.

cases.title: Migrate from Meerkat, Monica, or CRM-in-Markdown
cases.given: Synthetic Meerkat, Monica, and CRM-in-Markdown exports contain people, relationships, notes, activities, reminders, birthdays, custom data, and unsupported source fields.
cases.when: Each migration is previewed, identity decisions are confirmed, applied, rolled back, and re-imported.
cases.then: The reconciliation report maps imported, transformed, unresolved, and unsupported data; source IDs and provenance remain available; rollback restores the target; re-import is idempotent.

cases.title: Preview remote AI disclosure
cases.given: A remote AI grant covers selected profile fields for one meeting and expires tomorrow.
cases.when: The user opens the disclosure preview and submits the request.
cases.then: The preview and captured fixture payload match exactly, excluded fields and private overlays are absent, and the grant cannot be reused after expiry.

cases.title: Repair invalid external edit
cases.given: An external editor creates a duplicate person ID and invalid birthday.
cases.when: The administrator runs workspace validation and guided repair.
cases.then: Both defects are identified, repair is previewed, no file is silently deleted, and a backup and audit entry exist before changes are applied.

cases.title: Complete Workplace Review Alpha in the installed Mac app
cases.given: A fresh universal internal review build and deterministic 10,000-person workplace fixture are installed for one trusted workspace owner with network access denied.
cases.when: The receptionist completes Event Details, Cohort, Attendees, Readiness, and Brief; exercises every ordered readiness outcome and invalid combination; corrects duplicates, unresolved identities, cancellations, removals, walk-ins, attendance and no-shows; records only bounded EventReadinessFollowUp; previews disclosure; generates an immutable internal brief; and delivers CSV and HTML.
cases.then: Every lifecycle transition and active denominator reconcile exactly; preview and emitted bytes match; restricted fields, diagnoses, private notes, hidden-row counts, generic tasks, relationship allocation, cadence, scoring, and attention weights are structurally absent; delivery verifies without overwrite; and the declared keyboard, VoiceOver, contrast, 400 percent zoom and reflow, narrow-window, reduced-motion, localisation, interruption, offline, readable-file, and egress matrix passes.

cases.title: Recover authority and healthy records around failures
cases.given: One workspace has a malformed Person, an active writer, and injected operation crashes before and after the commit decision.
cases.when: A second process opens Health, attempts a mutation, and the first process relaunches.
cases.then: Health reports the malformed file, healthy People remain available, the second mutation receives a typed lock error, pre-commit staging is discarded, and committed work rolls forward without overwriting an external edit.

cases.title: Restore encrypted B0 recovery on a clean installation
cases.given: A B0 workspace contains sealed dietary data and the target Mac has no Liaison Keychain entry.
cases.when: The user restores an encrypted recovery package with its passphrase into an isolated directory.
cases.then: Manifest and ciphertext integrity verify, the workspace opens with authorised values available, a wrong passphrase exposes nothing, and the source workspace remains unchanged.

cases.title: Complete Personal Memory Alpha without regressing B0
cases.given: B0 is accepted and the same local workspace foundation is installed.
cases.when: The user exercises Personal, Family, Professional, and Networking workspace purposes; edits a profile; configures typed fields, tabs, settings, and appearance; records an interaction and bounded commitment; reviews factual reasons; relaunches; and inspects canonical files.
cases.then: Purpose boundaries, profile context, open loops, layouts, and supported appearance survive interruption and clean settings round trip in readable or sealed canonical forms; no relationship-strength or workplace score appears; and the full B0 event workflow still passes.

cases.title: Keep relationship contexts purpose-bound
cases.given: One workspace-local person has several effective-dated contexts and separate Personal, Family, Professional, Networking, and Workplace workspaces exist.
cases.when: The user searches, exports, reviews attention reasons, and optionally adds an opaque identity bridge.
cases.then: Same-purpose contexts remain distinct, cross-purpose workspaces share no facts or access, and the bridge changes no value, count, export, assessment, or policy.

cases.title: Manage a consent-aware Working With Me guide
cases.given: A person has a Shared Guide, private observations, and source records with different authorship evidence.
cases.when: The user creates, confirms, shares, expires, corrects, withdraws, and purges guide sections.
cases.then: Private observations and their existence remain excluded, consent and source state remain inspectable, and no label overstates authorship or identity proof.

cases.title: Update interaction-derived context deterministically
cases.given: A relationship has visible cadence, assessment, and no qualifying interaction.
cases.when: The user logs, corrects, and deletes a qualifying interaction.
cases.then: Last interaction, freshness, due reason, timeline, CLI, canonical files, and desktop update deterministically while manual assessment remains unchanged.

cases.title: Round-trip a promoted Markdown note
cases.given: A canonical note contains links, unknown Markdown, and no copied timeline body.
cases.when: The user creates, pins or promotes, externally edits, closes, and reopens it.
cases.then: One source record retains all content, the timeline links to it, and core navigation and profile layout remain stable.

cases.title: Save and transfer a grant-filtered professional view
cases.given: People have mixed organisation, relationship, profile, and grant states.
cases.when: The user searches, filters, inspects predicates, saves the view, and exports then imports its settings.
cases.then: The clean workspace reproduces the view and keyboard or screen-reader flow while ungranted records remain absent from UI, CLI, and export.

cases.title: Transfer settings without transferring records or secrets
cases.given: A settings bundle contains layouts, fields, packs, views, policies, appearance, unknown safe keys, and adversarial record, path, and secret-like literals.
cases.when: The user validates, previews, dry-runs, applies, conflicts, rolls back, and imports it on a clean device.
cases.then: Only classified settings round-trip; unsafe literals are rejected or redacted; personal records, identifiers, secrets, tokens, caches, and device paths are absent; rollback restores the prior revision.

cases.title: Use transparent personal allocation without workplace scoring
cases.given: A self-owned non-Workplace relationship context has explicit sources and suppressions.
cases.when: The user versions and previews an allocation policy and then opens equivalent Workplace fixtures.
cases.then: The personal result explains every input and suppression and no global person score exists; Workplace files, UI, CLI, settings, exports, and adapters reject allocation and relationship-value fields.

cases.title: Keep relationship reminders bounded
cases.given: Allowed relationship sources, reasons, and recurrence rules are defined.
cases.when: The user exercises every allowed reminder transition and attempts arbitrary tasks, assignees, checklists, dependencies, projects, workloads, boards, and generic recurrence.
cases.then: Allowed states remain source-linked and recoverable while every generic task or project field is rejected in files, CLI, UI, and reusable later API or plugin fixtures.

cases.title: Merge and split duplicate people without loss
cases.given: Two people contain overlapping source IDs, relationships, notes, contexts, and provenance plus unresolved identities.
cases.when: The administrator previews and applies a merge, then splits the result.
cases.then: All accepted facts and provenance return to inspectable owners, no link is lost or duplicated, and unresolved identities remain quarantined and visible.

cases.title: Remove a cross-workspace identity bridge without leakage
cases.given: Two purpose-bound workspaces contain opaque references to the same person but no shared facts.
cases.when: The owner adds and removes the optional bridge and repeats search, count, export, assessment, and attention operations.
cases.then: No fact or authority crosses workspaces and bridge removal leaves no ordinary-export or query trace.

cases.title: Prevent deleted sensitive data from returning after restore
cases.given: Sensitive data is deleted and purged after an older checkpoint or recovery package was created.
cases.when: The older snapshot is restored in isolation and authenticated deletion records are replayed through compaction and backup-expiry rules.
cases.then: Neither payload nor identifying metadata reactivates, becomes searchable, or leaks through counts, errors, audit, export, or recovery evidence.

cases.title: Label Working With Me authorship accurately
cases.given: A guide has locally witnessed subject confirmation plus unsigned, valid signed, invalid signed, and owner-observed sections.
cases.when: The records are imported, corrected, shared, and reopened.
cases.then: Each section retains an accurate source and approval label, invalid signatures cannot verify authorship, and private observations remain private.

cases.title: Discover source-linked network context without scoring
cases.given: A narrow grant permits factual sources for one user goal and denies sensitive inference.
cases.when: The user requests network discovery and the adapter proposes closeness, loyalty, employee value, or likelihood in adversarial fixtures.
cases.then: Permitted suggestions link to exact sources and prohibited inferred rankings are rejected before display or persistence.

cases.title: Run governed n8n examples
cases.given: Versioned local API and webhook contracts have scoped credentials, destination grants, and idempotency keys.
cases.when: Packaged examples run allowed, denied, replayed, retried, redirected, and revoked cases.
cases.then: Only the allowed operation succeeds once and every denial or retry has bounded non-sensitive evidence.

cases.title: Capture an interaction by local voice
cases.given: Networking is denied and a local audio source is available.
cases.when: The user records voice, reviews the source, transcript, and structured interaction proposal, then confirms selected fields.
cases.then: No network request occurs, nothing persists before confirmation, and the retained or deleted source follows explicit policy.

cases.title: Capture a business card by local OCR
cases.given: Networking is denied and a synthetic business-card image contains duplicates and uncertain fields.
cases.when: The user previews mapping and identity matches, corrects the proposal, confirms selected fields, and chooses source retention.
cases.then: Only confirmed fields persist with provenance, duplicates remain inspectable, and source retention or deletion follows policy.

cases.title: Hand off follow-up without creating a task platform
cases.given: A scoped grant permits one relationship-follow-up envelope and read-only external status.
cases.when: The user sends it to Meitheal or another execution system and receives permitted status updates.
cases.then: The envelope and status remain source-linked and revocable while Liaison exposes no general assignment, dependency, routine, project, workload, or execution surface.

cases.title: Use every accessible built-in theme through the B0 journey
cases.given: The installed B0 app provides system, light, dark, and high_contrast built-ins through one semantic token and component contract.
cases.when: The user completes Details, Cohort, Attendees, Readiness, and Brief in each built-in, restarts, recovers, and changes the operating-system preference.
cases.then: Choice and OS-following persist; focus, contrast, non-colour state, 400 percent reflow, targets, localisation, and reduced-motion behaviour preserve meaning and action.

cases.title: Round-trip accessible appearance settings
cases.given: A0 exposes supported density, text scale, motion, and validated palette settings plus unknown safe keys.
cases.when: The user previews, applies, resets, exports, and imports them on a clean workspace by keyboard and screen reader.
cases.then: Supported and unknown-safe settings round-trip without record leakage or semantic loss, unsafe values are rejected, and reset restores the known-good built-in.

cases.title: Reject unsafe declarative theme packages
cases.given: Compatible, incompatible, corrupt, code-bearing, remote-asset, and malicious local theme packages are available.
cases.when: The user validates schema range, checksum, licence, semantic tokens, assets and capabilities, previews, activates, and rolls back.
cases.then: Only a passing package may activate with an accurate conformance label; all unsafe packages leave the active theme unchanged and rollback restores the last known-good built-in.

cases.title: Author and round-trip the pinned OKF People profile
cases.given: Every B0-released UI and CLI Person writer uses one typed adapter, and fixtures include unknown fields, unknown sections, ordinary links, curated body bytes, domain extensions, and sealed sensitive values.
cases.when: The operator creates and edits People through every released path, validates the pinned OKF v0.1 Draft profile, changes one controlled field, and round-trips each document through an ordinary Markdown tool.
cases.then: Every non-reserved document has a non-empty type person envelope, mapped core fields and stable Liaison domain extensions; unknown fields, sections, body bytes and links are preserved, and sealed values never appear in plaintext. A0 reuses this case only as a regression dependency and does not co-own it.

cases.title: Normalize and exactly roll back legacy People files
cases.given: A legacy workspace includes valid People, malformed siblings, unknown extensions, ordinary broken links, curated index content, reserved names, and fault injection at every write boundary.
cases.when: The operator previews the OKF People normalization, creates the exact backup, commits, interrupts and restarts at each boundary, reruns the operation, and performs exact rollback.
cases.then: The journal provides failure-atomic recovery, rerun is idempotent, rollback restores byte-exact originals, stable IDs and links survive, curated indexes are not overwritten, malformed siblings remain inspectable, healthy People remain available, and no partial profile or index state exists.

cases.title: Use the source-complete purpose-scoped profile end to end
cases.given: A purpose-scoped Person has sourced current, stale, conflicting, withheld and proposed facts, memberships, a note, an interaction, and a custom profile field.
cases.when: The user completes quick and full capture, finds and edits key context, inspects source, freshness, conflict and correction history, logs a Markdown note, moves a field to a custom tab, reopens from disk, and repeats the critical path by keyboard, VoiceOver and 400 percent zoom and reflow.
cases.then: The accessible overview and stable tabs preserve all facts and source states, last note and last interaction remain distinct, unknown safe material survives, and no global person score or silent external-edit winner exists.

cases.title: Review identity candidates without automatic merge
cases.given: Exact shared identifiers, ambiguous identifiers, fuzzy matches, prior rejection, and divergent field and source histories produce identity candidates.
cases.when: The user inspects candidate reasons and field or source differences, remembers a rejection, previews and cancels a merge, merges, undoes, and splits the records.
cases.then: Every operation is explicit and reversible, source IDs and histories survive, remembered rejection persists, and neither exact identifiers nor fuzzy thresholds automatically merge People.

cases.title: Inspect a source- and range-labelled unified timeline
cases.given: A local Person has notes, interactions, meetings and events plus one bounded imported source with partial coverage and corrected rows.
cases.when: The user filters the unified timeline by kind, source and date and inspects direction, channel, participants, source, requested and covered range, skipped rows, correction history and completeness.
cases.then: Local and imported items remain source-linked and expandable, coverage limitations are explicit, corrections remain inspectable, factual counts remain factual, and no activity count becomes a global person score.

cases.title: Inspect and revoke a provider or import operation
cases.given: A connection has a purpose grant, connector version, requested source and range, successful and failed attempts, counts, conflicts, errors, retention and history.
cases.when: The user inspects the operations feed, retries a failed operation, revokes the grant, and observes subsequent scheduled time.
cases.then: Receipts expose attempt and success times plus created, updated, skipped and quarantined counts; retry and revoke are auditable, no hidden refresh or unreported provider egress occurs, and operation state never pollutes relationship reminders.

cases.title: Review a source-backed enrichment proposal
cases.given: A life-change or biography signal has old and new values, a source, confidence, egress context, and conflicts with a confirmed fact.
cases.when: The user reviews, rejects, corrects, and later accepts a staged proposal through the application service.
cases.then: No AI, MCP, plugin, provider or import path directly overwrites the confirmed fact, changes assessment or freshness, resets cadence, alters disclosure, or affects operational readiness before explicit valid acceptance.

cases.title: Use optional spatial discovery without hidden geocoding
cases.given: A non-Workplace workspace contains coarse and precise locations, a denied geocoder, an explicitly granted geocoder, corrected coordinates, and a matching semantic list or table.
cases.when: The user searches with networking denied, previews and grants geocoding egress, corrects and deletes coordinates, compares the semantic result, and attempts the same feature in Workplace purpose.
cases.then: Local and granted results are equivalent to the list or table, all egress is disclosed and revocable, coordinate deletion persists, Workplace denies the capability structurally, and no relationship score or hidden sync ranks People.

cases.title: Preserve the existing desktop use cases through the P04 contract
cases.given: The same synthetic workspace and exact generated command contract are available to the legacy and replacement desktop clients.
cases.when: The operator checks status, creates or opens a workspace, reloads and resumes its writer session, creates and lists one Person, triggers a typed failure, runs read-only Health, and closes the workspace.
cases.then: Both clients produce equivalent safe Application results and accessible state, retain one writer authority without duplicate mutation, preserve the draft on failure, and expose only Overview, Workspace, People, and Health.

cases.title: Review the P04 shell in every transient built-in at 400 percent zoom
cases.given: The exact P04 shell uses synthetic P02 states with system, light, dark, and high_contrast transient previews, en-XA, reduced motion, a named screen reader, and 320 or 360 CSS pixel reflow at 400 percent zoom.
cases.when: The user traverses Overview, Workspace, People, and Health by keyboard, changes operating-system appearance, previews and cancels each built-in, and exercises empty, loading, error, success, conflict, and recovery states.
cases.then: No primary action or information is clipped or colour-only, focus and announcements remain logical, preview cancellation and fail-closed fallback restore the prior rendering, no external request occurs, and the result makes no persistence or complete-B0 claim.
